source.title: Fire and Platform Evacuation - VK 900 A Platform

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Viosca Knoll Block 900

actions_taken: Firefighting with handheld and wheel unit extinguishers
All 6 POB evacuated to M/V Landon James
Personnel transported to MP 296 B for medical evaluation
Fire extinguished by M/V Ms. Emily and M/V Mr. Cade using water cannons
All POB transported to onshore medical facility
event.summary: On 22 July 2023 at approximately 1240 hours, a fire occurred on VK 900 A Platform when diesel fuel from a tote tank on the top deck spilled onto the hot turbine compressor intake housing on the production deck below. The spill occurred during a diesel transfer operation when operators left the discharge hose in the containment skid during lunch break, causing a siphoning effect that overflowed the containment. Despite firefighting efforts by 6 POB using handheld and wheel unit extinguishers, the fire spread to the 8-man sleeper and grew beyond control. The PIC ordered evacuation at approximately 1320 hours. All personnel safely evacuated via M/V Landon James with no serious injuries. Two supply vessels extinguished the fire using water cannons. Estimated damage: $1.9 million.
key_phrases: diesel spill onto hot turbine exhaust
siphoning effect from discharge hose placement

hazards.name: Electrical energy from damaged extension cord

threats.name: Uncontrolled siphoning of diesel from day tank
Overflow of containment skid during fuel transfer
Diesel contact with hot turbine exhaust surface
Diesel contact with exposed electrical wiring
Inadequate hazard awareness during fuel transfer operations

consequences.name: Fire on production deck at turbine compressor
Fire spread to top deck diesel tote tanks

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for diesel transfer operations
controls.lod_basis: Frontline work planning and hazard identification

supporting_text: BSEE requested Cox provide the Job Safety Analysis's (JSA) used for the maintenance work performed on the Firewater/Jockey Pump, the chemical transfer process, and any other JSA performed on 22 July 2023. Cox was unable to provide the requested JSA's, stating that they were 'lost in the fire'.
Management Systems – No or inadequate hazard analysis/written job procedure: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for the transfer of diesel was not found.

controls.name: Containment skid for diesel tote tanks
controls.lod_basis: Passive engineered barrier to contain leaks/spills

supporting_text: According to Cox's report, the discharge hose was removed from the tote tank and placed within the containment skid, and the suction hose was left in the day tank while operators broke for lunch. It is suspected that the vacuum created by placing the discharge hose on the grating caused a siphoning effect from the day tank, resulting in diesel spilling out and overflowing the containment skid.

controls.name: Separation of ignition sources from flammable materials

supporting_text: Investigators discovered two possible ignition sources. The first was the turbine compressor exhaust, which was situated below diesel tote tanks that were stored on the top deck directly above.
This caused the diesel to flow onto the hot turbine exhaust and exposed extension cord, which may have caused ignition.

controls.name: Electrical equipment integrity (extension cord condition)
controls.barrier_role: Equipment integrity to prevent electrical ignition

supporting_text: Evidence suggests that the extension cord running from the transfer pump to the 8-man sleeper could be another contributing cause to the incident. The extension cord going to the pump from the 8-man sleeper was laid on top of the grating. Signs of oxidation and decay were observed on the extension cord, suggesting that the cord had been stepped on while in use.
It is crucial to note that the extension cord's exposed wiring could have potentially caused ignition when the diesel spilled out of the containment skid.

controls.name: Operator supervision and monitoring of fuel transfer
controls.barrier_role: Human monitoring to detect and respond to abnormal conditions
controls.lod_basis: Active human monitoring during critical operations

supporting_text: According to Cox's report, the discharge hose was removed from the tote tank and placed within the containment skid, and the suction hose was left in the day tank while operators broke for lunch.
Human Performance Error – Not aware of hazards: Personnel left the hose used for the diesel transfer and extension cord out while they broke for lunch.

controls.name: Fire detection system and alarm
controls.lod_basis: First line of defense for consequence mitigation

supporting_text: On 22 July 2023, at approximately 1240 hours, witnesses stated hearing 'Fire' over the radio followed by the fire alarm sounding.
The PIC checked the Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) computer and saw that the alarm was from the #1 turbine compressor.
In response, the platform's emergency shut down (ESD) system was manually initiated, and the PIC and other POB grabbed handheld fire extinguishers to fight the fire at the compressor.

controls.name: Portable fire extinguishers (handheld and wheel units)
controls.lod_basis: Manual firefighting equipment for initial response

supporting_text: POB attempted to extinguish the fire using handheld and wheel unit fire extinguishers. Despite their firefighting efforts, the fire quickly grew beyond their control
The fire at the turbine compressor was eventually contained, but upstairs at the diesel tote tanks, the fire was burning out of control and quickly spread to the 8-man sleeper

POB continued using handheld and wheel unit fire extinguishers to fight the fire but were unsuccessful. After exhausting most of the fire extinguishers onboard, the PIC ordered all 6 POB to evacuate the platform.

The Operator stated that during the morning of the incident, he and another operator were tasked with transferring diesel from a day tank into an empty tote tank due to sludge found in the tank. The sludge in the day tank was causing the fuel filters on the generator powering the Firewater/Jockey Pump to clog. As a result, the Firewater/Jockey Pump was shut down at 1600 on 21 July 2023.

controls.name: Emergency evacuation procedures and muster
controls.lod_basis: Last line of defense for personnel safety

supporting_text: After exhausting most of the fire extinguishers onboard, the PIC ordered all 6 POB to evacuate the platform.
POB made their way to the muster station and donned their personal floatation devices.
At approximately 1320 hours, all POB safely boarded the responding Motor Vessel (M/V) Landon James

controls.name: Emergency response vessels with firefighting capability

supporting_text: At approximately 1330 hours, M/V Ms. Emily and M/V Mr. Cade arrived on scene and assisted extinguishing the fire using their vessel's water cannons.
Both vessels remained on location until approximately 1500 hours on 23 July 2023.
witnesses stated hearing 'Fire' over the radio followed by the fire alarm sounding
Through the duration of the incident, the PIC remained in contact with the Area Foreman and transportation for the evacuation was initiated.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
